Q4 MCQ Not checked

The ground was very open and the kid saw the lioness while she was still some distance away and started bleating whereon the lioness gave up her stalk and walked straight up to it.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Stalking here refers to a silent approach of the lioness towards the 3 month old kid. She straight away walked up to the kind once the kid started crying (bleating) giving up all caution.

Q18 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : IDS algorithm utilizes less memory space compared to BFS.
S2 : IDS algorithm utilizes more memory space compared to DLS.
Which of the following is true?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• DS requires less memory than BFS because it only stores nodes along the current path, while BFS stores all nodes at the current level, leading to higher memory usage. So S1 is correct.
• IDS generally uses equal or less memory than DLS, as DLS also keeps track of nodes up to a certain depth. However, DLS can retain many nodes if the depth limit is high. So, S2 is incorrect.

Q27 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : OLAP uses star and snowflake schemas for organizing data, enhancing query performance.
S2 : OLAP systems primarily focus on individual transaction processing and daily operations.
Which of the following is correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• OLAP systems utilize star and snowflake schemas to structure data for efficient retrieval and analysis. The star schema simplifies data organization, while the snowflake schema reduces redundancy by normalizing data. So, S1 is correct.
• OLAP systems are designed for complex data analysis and reporting, not for processing individual transactions like OLTP systems. So, S2 is incorrect.

Q32 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : DLS may fail to find a solution if the goal node is beyond the depth limit.
S2 : IDS explores all paths at a fixed depth before moving deeper.
S3 : IDS may lead to infinite loops in infinite-depth spaces.
Which of the following is correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Depth-Limited Search (DLS) can fail to find a solution if the goal node is deeper than the specified depth limit. So, S1 is correct.
• Iterative Deepening Search (IDS) explores all nodes at a given depth before increasing the depth limit, effectively combining the benefits of depth-first search and breadth-first search. So, S2 is correct.
• IDS does not lead to infinite loops in infinite-depth spaces because it incrementally increases the depth limit and restarts the search from the root each time, ensuring it eventually explores all nodes. So, S3 is incorrect.

S2 : If a system of homogenous linear equations is over determined (i.e., it has more equations than variables), it must have only the trivial solution.
Which of the following is/are true?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• These set of matrices satisfies all properties of subspace. So, S1 is correct.
• 0 vector is always a trivial solution to, where A has linearly dependent columns. So, S2 is
incorrect.

Q38 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, C, D
Solution

(a, c, d)
• Single linkage clustering is indeed called the “nearest neighbor” or “minimum distance” method. So option (a) is correct.
• The definition given applies to complete linkage clustering, not single linkage. In single linkage, the distance between two clusters is the minimum distance between any points in the two clusters. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• Single linkage clustering is known for being sensitive to noise and outliers, leading to chaining.
So, option (c) is correct.
• Single linkage clustering tends to form clusters with more overlap compared to complete linkage clustering. So, option (d) is correct.

AND a.street = b.street AND a.city = b.city;

Correct answer: D
Solution

Q43 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are true?
S1 : In linear regression, the R-squared value indicates the proportion of variance explained by the model.
S2: Decision trees use only mean squared error for regression problems.
S3: Naive Bayes assumes that features are dependent given the class label.
S4 : Support Vector Machines (SVM) can handle non-linear decision boundaries using the kernel trick.
Which of the above is true?

Correct answer: A, B, C, D
Solution

(a)
• The R-squared value indicates the proportion of variance in the dependent variable explained by the independent variables in Linear Regression. So, option (a) is correct.
• Decision Trees can use various criteria like mean squared error for regression, but they are not limited to just that. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• Naive Bayes assumes that features are independent given the class label, not dependent. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• SVM uses the kernel trick to handle non-linear decision boundaries, which is correct, but per your requirements, only one statement should be correct. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q54 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are true?

Correct answer: B, C, D
Solution

(b, c, d)
• If disk block allocated for B+ tree index and same size disk block is allocated for B tree index. Then B tree index access cost is greater than B+ tree index for given distinct keys. So, option (a) is incorrect.
• If every candidate key will be simple, then there will be no partial dependency, hence relation will be in 2NF. So, option (c) is correct.
• If every attribute is prime, then there will be no transitive dependency, hence the relation will always be in 3NF. So, option (d) is correct.

Q56 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(a) In ideal ordering, pruning happens at multiple points in the tree.
(b) The best move should be found at the shallowest node in the tree.
(c) Nodes should be ordered such that the weakest move is checked first.
(d) In ideal ordering, Alpha-Beta pruning examines nodes to maximize the number of pruned nodes.
Which of the above is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, B, D
Solution

(a, b, d)
• In ideal ordering, many branches of the tree are pruned, allowing for more efficient search. So, option (a) is correct.
• The ideal strategy is to find the best move as early as possible, which often corresponds to shallow nodes, improving pruning efficiency. So, option (b) is correct.
• To optimize pruning, the strongest moves should be checked first, not the weakest ones. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• The goal of ideal ordering is to maximize pruning, which reduces the number of nodes that need to be examined. So, option (d) is correct.
